stance: Calls for a 'true budget investigation' through an AI audit of city finances.
quote: true budget investigation through AI audit

stance: Top priority is changing Reno's 'weak mayor' system, arguing the city has outgrown its current government structure.
quote: Changing weak mayor system

stance: Identifies mental health and addiction as primary causes of homelessness requiring support-based responses.
quote: mental health issues, as well as addiction

stance: Pledges to continue opposing data centers and Flock surveillance cameras.
quote: continue to oppose flock cameras and data centers

stance: Says Reno needs more genuinely affordable housing via smart development and long-term planning, not short-term fixes.
quote: Reno needs more housing that people can actually afford. I'll support smart development, reduce bottlenecks, and ensure long-term planning, not short-term fixes.

stance: Says city government must be accountable; would hold himself and council members responsible for listening to constituents.
quote: Reno's government must be accountable to the community that it represents

stance: Backs smart, balanced growth funded by restricted utility funds rather than the General Fund, with regionalization to cut redundancy.
quote: Utilize restricted funds for utilities and stop abusing the General Fund

stance: Promises transparency, ending hidden liabilities, and bringing stakeholders into the conversation.
quote: Be transparent, stop the hidden liabilities and answer 'Why?'

stance: Calls the homelessness system a record of exhausted failures, with thousands stuck in a fragmented, uncoordinated system.
quote: exhausted failures. Thousands are stuck in the fragmented system

stance: Highlights that over half of Reno renters are cost-burdened and backs fair housing, eviction transparency and affordable-housing funding.
quote: Over 50% of renters are cost-burdened

stance: Frames the budget as a statement of priorities and says he is not interested in cutting city staff.
quote: Our budget is a reflection of our priorities

stance: Says the city must keep people from falling into homelessness and help them out of it, treating it as a public-health and public-safety issue.
quote: We also must do what we can to make sure people don't fall through the cracks and into homelessness, and try to help them out of it when we do.

stance: Pledges to keep working to make housing and childcare affordable, citing affordable units built and preserved during his tenure.
quote: We need to continue to do everything in our power to help make it so that housing, childcare, and everything else is actually affordable.

stance: Supports a community policing model with neighborhood police stations across the city.
quote: I want to see community police stations spread across the city, to provide better service and to continue our move toward a community public safety model of neighborhood policing.

stance: Supports a moratorium to slow data-center approvals while sustainable, forward-thinking regulations are created.
quote: We need to slow down approval of data centers and I support adopting a moratorium that allows time to create sustainable, forward-thinking regulations.

stance: Would prioritize development in blighted areas, vacant buildings and infill while protecting historic character and open space.
quote: We should prioritize development in blighted areas, vacant buildings and appropriate infill sites while protecting historic character and open spaces.

stance: Says raising taxes on small businesses and nonprofits will not solve the city's fiscal crisis.
quote: Simply raising taxes on small businesses and non-profits will not get us out of this crisis.

stance: Calls for leaders who disclose who is influencing their decision-making.
quote: We need leaders who are transparent and disclose who is influencing their decision making.

stance: Would eliminate wasteful and duplicate regional spending and sell or lease underutilized city assets to cut municipal debt.
quote: Eliminate wasteful spending and duplicate funding of regional services

stance: Would never cut public-safety funding, calling police, fire and emergency services core local-government responsibilities.
quote: I would never cut funding for public safety. Police, fire and emergency services are the core responsibilities of local government and essential to protecting lives.

stance: Wants a balanced housing mix that keeps Reno affordable while respecting current residents' quality of life.
quote: Balancing the need for housing with community concerns begins with respecting our current residents by ensuring that new development does not negatively impact their quality of life.

stance: Says data centers should fully mitigate water and power impacts and be compatible with surrounding land uses.
quote: These projects should be required to fully mitigate their impact on critical infrastructure, including water and power, and be designed in a way that is compatible with surrounding land uses.

stance: Says Reno needs more housing, citing unambiguous data on affordability, workforce retention and regional growth.
quote: We need more housing. The data on affordability, workforce retention and regional growth is unambiguous.

stance: Wants growth that works for current residents, with infrastructure planned before projects are approved.
quote: growth that works for the people who already live here, instead of approving projects first

stance: Frames parks and preventive maintenance as health and cost-saving infrastructure; says prevention is far cheaper than deferred fixes.
quote: Prevention costs pennies. Treatment costs millions.

stance: Would require data centers to use air-based, non-water cooling systems except for immediate fire hazards.
quote: We should make sure that they have non-water cooling/heating systems instead using air for cooling and heating except for immediate fire hazards.

stance: Would tax landlords on empty units and limit out-of-state in-migration until reaching a 3:1 housing-availability ratio.
quote: By taxing landlords about empty housing that they have and try to stop people from coming in out of state until we have a higher, preferably 3:1 housing availability.

stance: Would cut the city council's pension (net of basic living costs) but says he would never cut park funding.
quote: I'd like to cut the city council's pension minus their bills for rent/mortgage and groceries. Something I would never cut is funding for city parks.

stance: Names a lack of infrastructure to support residents, including first responders and roads, as the city's biggest problem; backs responsible development.
Says high-demand projects like data centers must prove they won't burden residents and meet clear water, traffic and safety standards.
quote: if a project can't meet clear standards for traffic, water availability, and public safety, it shouldn't move forward

stance: Calls water the most limited resource in Northern Nevada and says decisions made as if it's unlimited are reckless.
Pledges transparent budgeting, cutting government waste and decisions that are explained rather than buried in reports.
quote: I promise to be guided by common sense. I'll be transparent, and I'll listen to you

stance: Would offer services first but use criminal law against those violating public health and safety.
quote: We're going to compassionately try to connect you to services. But if you are violating public health and public safety, you're going to use the criminal law.

stance: Says he wants to make Washoe safer and restore the integrity of the DA's office, citing uncharged cases under the incumbent.
quote: make Washoe County a safer place and restore the integrity of the DA's office

stance: Prioritizes student mental health and wellness via expanded counseling, early intervention and whole-child support.
quote: Expanding school-based counseling, early intervention, and whole-child support.

stance: Backs a classroom-first approach with budget transparency so more dollars reach teachers and students.
Says being upfront and straightforward about decisions builds trust on the board.
quote: Being upfront and straightforward about decisions helps build trust

stance: Says transparency on any deficit is critical, emphasizing open communication and community feedback.
quote: Transparency on any deficit is also critical.
